papers.title_en: OpenRTLSet: A Fully Open-Source Dataset for Large Language Model-based Verilog Module Design
papers.authors: Jinghua Wang, Lily Jiaxin Wan, Sanjana Pingali, Scott Smith, Manvi Jha, Shalini Sivakumar, Xing Zhao, Kaiwen Cao, Deming Chen
papers.evidence: 为基于LLM的Verilog模块设计提供大规模开源数据集，支持RTL代码生成
papers.tldr: 硬件设计领域缺乏大规模开源数据集，现有资源往往规模小或受专有限制。为此，我们构建了OpenRTLSet，一个包含131k+多样Verilog代码样本的全开源数据集，涵盖GitHub仓库、VHDL翻译和C/C++翻译。利用DeepSeek-R1推理模型为每个样本生成自然语言描述，并微调Qwen、Granite等语言模型。实验表明，开源方法在Verilog生成任务上优于闭源方案，为硬件设计的研究与商业应用建立了可访问的新基础。
papers.abstract_en: OpenRTLSet introduces the largest fully open-source dataset for hardware design, offering over 131,000 diverse Verilog code samples to the research community and industry. Our dataset uniquely combines Verilog code from GitHub repositories (102k modules), VHDL translations (5k modules), and synthesizable C/C++ translations (24k modules), all freely accessible without proprietary restrictions. Using the reasoning model DeepSeek-R1, we generated paired natural language descriptions for each code sample, enabling fine-tuning of various language model families (e.g., Qwen and Granite) for Verilog code generation. Our dataset explores multiple options, including Verilator-generated C++ files as additional context during labeling, quantization techniques (INT4 vs. BF16), and performance differences across model sizes (7B-32B parameters). OpenRTLSet demonstrates that open-source approaches can achieve superior performance in hardware design tasks, establishing a new foundation for accessible research and commercial use in this domain.
